title: hieracium piloselloides subsp. florentinum
intro: Hieracium piloselloides subsp. florentinum, also known as a sub-species of Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hieracium piloselloides subsp. florentinum include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Hieracium piloselloides subsp. florentinum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical cream, although its use as a medicinal herb should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
